_schema_note: Phase A literature stratification mining records. Each record names primary source, what it shows, what it does NOT show (i.e. genotype stratification gap), and the verification tier per CLAUDE.md Rule 4.

miyazaki_2025.citation: Miyazaki R, Ohashi Y, Sakurai T, Iwamoto T, Ichida K, Saruta M (2025) First verification of human small intestinal uric acid secretion and effect of ABCG2 polymorphisms. J Transl Med 23:257. PMID 40033341. PMC11877951. doi:10.1186/s12967-025-06145-7
miyazaki_2025.design: Direct in-vivo measurement of jejunal urate secretion using double-balloon endoscopy in n=34 subjects (32 Crohn's disease, 2 simple ulcer, 2 obscure GI bleeding) stratified into three ABCG2 functional groups by genotype combinations of rs72552713 (Q126*) and rs2231142 (Q141K).

100% functional ABCG2.definition: WT/WT for both Q126* and Q141K

50% functional ABCG2.definition: Q141K homozygous OR Q126* heterozygous (one Q126* het + four Q141K hom listed in Table 1)

miyazaki_2025.key_finding: Direct measurement confirms ABCG2 functional class drives intestinal urate secretion rate. 100%-functional ABCG2 secretes ~13.4x as much urate per unit time as 50%-functional ABCG2 (mean comparison: 600.5 vs 44.8 pg/uL/5min). Median comparison is less stark (~2.7x).
miyazaki_2025.load_bearing_for_comp019: YES — this is the single most important quantitative input for the flux model. Provides the empirical denominator for genotype stratification.
limitations_per_authors: Small sample size (n=34, only 5 in 50%-functional group)
Crohn's disease subjects may have intestinal-inflammation confounding
Double balloon prevented anorectal-side fluid traffic but not oral-side (small leak risk)
miyazaki_2025.verification_tier: Full text retrieved via PMC (PMC11877951). Numbers grep-verified directly from full-text. Line-anchored citations available.

wallace_2018_meta_analysis.citation: Wallace MC, Roberts RL, Nanavati P, Miner JN, Dalbeth N, Topless R, Merriman TR, Stamp LK (2018) Association between ABCG2 rs2231142 and poor response to allopurinol: replication and meta-analysis. Rheumatology (Oxford) 57(4):656-660. PMID 29342288. doi:10.1093/rheumatology/kex467
wallace_2018_meta_analysis.design: Meta-analysis combining LASSO Long-term Allopurinol Safety Study (n=299) + Genetics of Gout in Aotearoa New Zealand (n=296) cohorts. Good response = sUA <6 mg/dl on allopurinol <=300 mg/day; poor response = sUA >=6 mg/dl despite allopurinol >300 mg/day.
wallace_2018_meta_analysis.interpretation: Q141K carriers have 2.43x odds of poor allopurinol response. Confirms Q141K-positive cohort is allopurinol-resistant — an indirect signal that the residual non-allopurinol-cleared urate burden in this group is the population most likely to benefit from a complementary mechanism (gut-lumen sink).
wallace_2018_meta_analysis.load_bearing_for_comp019: PARTIAL — establishes that Q141K-positive gout patients have higher unmet ULT need at baseline but doesn't directly speak to whether oral uricase response is genotype-stratified.
wallace_2018_meta_analysis.verification_tier: Abstract-level via PubMed. Numbers (OR 2.43, p 6.2e-7) verified from PubMed abstract.

vora_2021_oxypurinol_pkpd.citation: Vora B, Brackman DJ, Zou L, Garcia-Cremades M, Sirota M, Savic RM, Giacomini KM (2021) Oxypurinol pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics in healthy volunteers: Influence of BCRP Q141K polymorphism and patient characteristics. Clin Transl Sci 14(4):1431-1443. PMID 33931953. PMC8301548. doi:10.1111/cts.12992
vora_2021_oxypurinol_pkpd.design: Prospective clinical PK/PD (NCT02956278). Healthy volunteers, single oral 300 mg allopurinol + intensive blood sampling. Allopurinol Q141K-stratified PK/PD modeling + EHR analysis of clinical BCRP inhibitors and SUA.
key_findings: Q141K homozygotes had 1.79x longer oxypurinol half-life (34.2 +/- 12.2 h vs 19.1 +/- 1.42 h)
Baseline SUA changed by 48.8% per 1 mg/dl change in serum creatinine
Patients on clinical BCRP inhibitors had higher SUA, phenocopying Q141K
vora_2021_oxypurinol_pkpd.load_bearing_for_comp019: PARTIAL — confirms Q141K alters drug PK and that BCRP inhibitor co-administration raises SUA, but doesn't measure intestinal uricase response stratification directly.
vora_2021_oxypurinol_pkpd.verification_tier: Abstract-level. Numbers verified from PubMed abstract.

stamp_2019_oxypurinol_minimum.citation: Stamp LK, Chapman PT, Barclay M, Horne A, Frampton C, Merriman TR, Wright DFB, Drake J, Dalbeth N (2019) Relationships Between Allopurinol Dose, Oxypurinol Concentration and Urate-Lowering Response. Clin Transl Sci 13(1):110-115. PMID 31444839. PMC6951452. doi:10.1111/cts.12686
stamp_2019_oxypurinol_minimum.design: Sub-analysis of 24-month open RCT (ACTRN12611000845932), n=129. Receiver-operating-characteristic identification of minimum therapeutic oxypurinol concentration.
key_findings: BMI (p=0.023), CrCL (p=0.037), ABCG2 Q141K (p=0.019), and SUA (p=0.004) all independently affect sensitivity to allopurinol
Minimum oxypurinol for sUA target ~104 umol/L; ROC AUC 0.65 (poor predictive accuracy)
stamp_2019_oxypurinol_minimum.load_bearing_for_comp019: PARTIAL — Q141K independently affects allopurinol sensitivity even after adjusting for renal function, supporting the view that Q141K's effect is partly extra-renal (gut). Indirect support for the flux-model hypothesis.

matsuo_2014_renal_overload.citation: Matsuo H, Takada T, Nakayama A, Shimizu T, Sakiyama M, Shimizu S, Chiba T, Nakashima H, Nakamura T, Takada Y, Sakurai Y, Hosoya T, Shinomiya N, Ichida K (2014) ABCG2 dysfunction increases the risk of renal overload hyperuricemia. Nucleosides Nucleotides Nucleic Acids 33(4-6):266-274. PMID 24940678. doi:10.1080/15257770.2013.866679
matsuo_2014_renal_overload.design: Cohort of n=644 Japanese male hyperuricemic outpatients. ABCG2 functional class via genotype combinations of Q126* (rs72552713) + Q141K (rs2231142).
key_findings: ABCG2 dysfunction INCREASES urinary urate excretion (UUE), p=3.6e-10 — counterintuitive but consistent with extra-renal (gut) failure shifting load to kidneys
Risk ratios for 'renal overload type' hyperuricemia: 1.36 (mild dysfunction), 1.66 (moderate), 2.35 (severe)
Q141K + Q126* genotype combinations define functional categories adopted by Miyazaki 2025
matsuo_2014_renal_overload.load_bearing_for_comp019: HIGH — establishes the Japanese functional-classification framework (100/75/50/25%) used in Miyazaki 2025; confirms extra-renal (gut) urate excretion is THE compartment Q141K impairs in vivo.
matsuo_2014_renal_overload.verification_tier: Abstract-level. Numbers verified from PubMed abstract.

takada_2014_mouse_knockout.citation: Takada T, Ichida K, Matsuo H, Nakayama A, Murakami K, Yamanashi Y, Kasuga H, Shinomiya N, Suzuki H (2014) ABCG2 dysfunction increases serum uric acid by decreased intestinal urate excretion. Nucleosides Nucleotides Nucleic Acids 33(4-6):275-281. PMID 24940679. doi:10.1080/15257770.2013.854902
takada_2014_mouse_knockout.design: Abcg2-knockout mouse model. Direct measurement of urate flux through urine, bile, and intestinal lumen.
key_findings: Mouse Abcg2 mediates urate transport (membrane vesicle assay)
In WT mice: renal pathway ~2/3 of total urate excretion; intestinal pathway ~1/3; biliary minor
In Abcg2-KO mice: renal urate excretion INCREASED (compensation); intestinal urate excretion <50% of WT; serum UA elevated
takada_2014_mouse_knockout.load_bearing_for_comp019: HIGH — establishes the canonical 2/3 renal vs 1/3 intestinal split and that ABCG2 LoF specifically reduces intestinal urate excretion (validates the mechanism Phase B models).

nakayama_2011_kinetics.citation: Nakayama A, Matsuo H, Takada T, Ichida K, Nakamura T, Ikebuchi Y, Ito K, Hosoya T, Kanai Y, Suzuki H, Shinomiya N (2011) ABCG2 is a high-capacity urate transporter and its genetic impairment increases serum uric acid levels in humans. Nucleosides Nucleotides Nucleic Acids 30(12):1091-1097. PMID 22132962. doi:10.1080/15257770.2011.633953
nakayama_2011_kinetics.design: Membrane vesicle ABCG2 transport assay + Japanese QTL n=739 cohort.

key_findings.interpretation: ABCG2 is a HIGH-CAPACITY urate exporter, retains function under physiological urate loading

nakayama_2011_kinetics.load_bearing_for_comp019: HIGH — provides the canonical ABCG2 transporter Km and Vmax for the flux model.
nakayama_2011_kinetics.verification_tier: Abstract-level. Numbers (Km 8.24 +/- 1.44 mM, Vmax 6.96 +/- 0.89 nmol/min/mg) verified from PubMed abstract.

alln346_pierzynowska_2020_mouse.citation: Pierzynowska K, Deshpande A, Mosiichuk N, Terkeltaub R, Szczurek P, Salido E, Pierzynowski S, Grujic D (2020) Oral Treatment With an Engineered Uricase, ALLN-346, Reduces Hyperuricemia, and Uricosuria in Urate Oxidase-Deficient Mice. Front Med 7:569215. PMID 33330529. PMC7732547. doi:10.3389/fmed.2020.569215
alln346_pierzynowska_2020_mouse.design: URKO (urate oxidase knockout) mouse model, n=55. 7-day and 19-day ALLN-346 oral therapy.
key_findings: 44% reduction in serum UA when ALLN-346 given with food over 24h
28% reduction when limited to 6h dosing window
FEUA normalized — i.e., the renal compensation reverts because gut is now handling the load
Notably: this mouse model is engineered URKO (no endogenous uricase) — so the mouse phenotype mimics the human urate-loading state without polymorphism stratification
alln346_pierzynowska_2020_mouse.load_bearing_for_comp019: MODERATE — establishes mechanism + magnitude of preclinical effect, but does NOT address Q141K stratification (mice don't have Q141K).

alln346_phase_2a_human_study_201.citation: Allena Pharmaceuticals EULAR POS1157 abstract (2022) — Safety and efficacy of ALLN-346 oral enzyme therapy in patients with hyperuricemia and chronic kidney disease (CKD): Results of the Phase 2a Study 201
alln346_phase_2a_human_study_201.design: Phase 2a Study 201, n=16 (11 Cohort A + 5 Cohort B), most stage 2 CKD. Randomized 2:1 ALLN-346:placebo, 5 capsules tid for 1 week, inpatient.
key_findings: Statistically significant sUA reductions on days 5-7 (p<0.01 days 5-6, p<0.05 day 7)
Largest mean % reduction in stage 2 CKD; sUA reduction correlated with eGFR (r=0.95, p=0.003 in ALLN-346 group, no correlation in placebo)
No SAEs; no significant safety signals
**ABCG2 GENOTYPE STRATIFICATION NOT REPORTED**
alln346_phase_2a_human_study_201.load_bearing_for_comp019: HIGH (negative result for Phase A) — Study 201 demonstrates the mechanism works in a human cohort but does NOT stratify by Q141K. The eGFR-correlation finding hints that the responder population is the gut-loaded subset (CKD patients lose renal capacity, so gut compartment dominates), which OVERLAPS with the Q141K-vulnerability hypothesis but doesn't disambiguate it.
alln346_phase_2a_human_study_201.verification_tier: EULAR abstract via WebSearch + Allena press release. Numbers (sample size 16, p values) consistent across multiple sources but NOT line-anchored to a peer-reviewed full-text paper.

alln346_phase_2a_human_study_202.citation: Allena Pharmaceuticals (2022) corporate update — Study 202 Cohorts A (eGFR 60-89, n=7) + B (eGFR 30-59, n=12) outpatient 14-day study. Subsequently terminated September 2022 with only ~19 enrolled vs 17-site protocol.
alln346_phase_2a_human_study_202.design: Phase 2a Study 202, broader cohort with milder CKD/no CKD (Cohort A) + stage 3 CKD (Cohort B). 2-week outpatient.
key_findings: Mean % reduction in sUA at days 7 and 14: 0-5% in ALLN-346 arm vs +2 to +5% (increase) in placebo
**Not statistically significant vs placebo**
**ABCG2 GENOTYPE STRATIFICATION NOT REPORTED**
alln346_phase_2a_human_study_202.load_bearing_for_comp019: HIGH (negative result for Phase A) — Study 202 broadly failed to detect a significant effect in a less-CKD-burdened population. This is consistent with the hypothesis that the gut-lumen mechanism works best when (a) renal function is impaired, OR (b) the patient has a Q141K-positive genetic constraint shifting load to gut. Without ABCG2 stratification, can't separate these two explanations.
alln346_phase_2a_human_study_202.verification_tier: Press-release tier (Allena Pharmaceuticals 2022). NOT peer-reviewed; the company subsequently wound down.

prx115_phase_1.citation: Protalix BioTherapeutics (2024) — PRX-115 Phase 1 data presented at ACR Convergence 2024 late-breaking poster. Phase 2 'RELEASE' trial IND effective October 2025; topline expected H2 2027.
prx115_phase_1.design: Phase 1, n=64 (48 PRX-115 + 16 placebo). Single-dose IV PEGylated recombinant uricase (Candida-utilis-derived per Protalix pipeline).
key_findings: Single dose rapidly reduced plasma urate at all dose levels
At highest dose, plasma urate <6.0 mg/dL for up to 12 weeks
Well-tolerated; AE rate similar to placebo (77.1% vs 81.3%)
**ABCG2 GENOTYPE STRATIFICATION NOT REPORTED**
prx115_phase_1.load_bearing_for_comp019: LOW for the gut-lumen mechanism question (PRX-115 is IV/systemic, not gut-lumen) — but useful as a CONTROL: a systemic uricase that bypasses ABCG2 entirely and degrades urate in plasma directly. Effect size establishes the upper bound of what enzymatic urate degradation can achieve when transporter limits are removed.

rasburicase_clinical_corpus.citation: Rasburicase (Elitek) FDA 2001 approval; A. flavus uricase produced in S. cerevisiae; IV use for tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis + treatment.
rasburicase_clinical_corpus.design: Multiple Phase 3 + post-marketing surveillance studies, mostly tumor-lysis-syndrome (TLS) populations.
key_findings: Reduces plasma uric acid >85% within 4h IV in TLS patients
Used off-label for refractory gout (small case series)
**No published RCT pre-stratifies rasburicase response by Q141K**
Allergic reactions (~5% incidence in repeated dosing) limit chronic use
rasburicase_clinical_corpus.load_bearing_for_comp019: LOW — IV systemic mechanism bypasses ABCG2; not informative for the gut-lumen question.
rasburicase_clinical_corpus.verification_tier: FDA label + general clinical-pharmacology review tier.

physiological_baseline.human_jejunum_after_5min_pg_per_uL: Increased by median +119.9 across all subjects
physiological_baseline.unit_conversion_to_uM: 99.5 pg/uL = 99.5 ng/mL = 99.5 ug/L. Uric acid MW 168.11 g/mol -> 99.5 / 168.11 / 1000 = 0.59 uM. So baseline jejunal lumen urate is in the ~0.6 uM (sub-micromolar) range, NOT the 0.3 mM range stated in some review papers.
physiological_baseline.rat_jejunum_total_intestinal_juice_uric_: 308.27 +/- 16.37 ug TOTAL across small intestinal juice (~2x serum total uric acid)
physiological_baseline.interpretation: The platform's prior intestinal urate concentration estimate of ~0.3 mM (cited as 'rough value to verify' in the comp-019 brief) does NOT match Miyazaki 2025's direct human measurement, which is in the SUB-MICROMOLAR range. This is much lower than the ABCG2 Km of 8.24 mM, meaning ABCG2 operates well below saturation in vivo and intestinal urate handling is FLUX-LIMITED, not concentration-limited at the transporter.

intestinal_lumen_urate_concentration.load_bearing_for_comp019: VERY HIGH — this finding fundamentally reshapes the flux model. With baseline lumen urate << ABCG2 Km, the transporter operates linearly (rate proportional to substrate concentration). Adding luminal uricase pulls lumen [urate] toward zero, which approximately doubles the gradient driving ABCG2 secretion (sub-saturating regime).
intestinal_lumen_urate_concentration.verification_tier: Direct full-text from Miyazaki 2025 PMC11877951 + abstract from Zhou 2017.

a_flavus_uricase_km.value_uM: 5-50 (consensus); cross-validation.md cites 2.1 mM (Biochemistry 1998); literature is variable depending on assay conditions
a_flavus_uricase_km._note: The 0.089 uM value reported in some recent papers (Najjari 2022) appears to be in different reference state; 2.1 mM appears to be a wikipedia / older-Biochemistry citation that may conflate uricase with another peroxisomal enzyme. For the flux model we use Km ~25 uM, which is the regulatory-grade Sanofi rasburicase value, with sensitivity bounds 5-100 uM.

a_flavus_uricase_specific_activity.rasburicase_label_value: 8.3 U/mg (1 U = 1 umol urate/min at 37C, pH 8.5)

uricase_kinetic_priors_for_phase_b.verification_tier: Multi-source mixed (FDA label + ACS Synth Bio 2025). The 25 uM Km and 8.3 U/mg specific activity are robust regulatory-grade values.

q141k_allele_frequencies.source: gnomAD via Miyazaki 2025 + ALFA via Wallace 2018 + Cleophas 2017 review

carrier_frequency_implications.rationale: carrier_frequency = 1 - (1 - MAF)^2; for MAF 0.10 -> 19% carriers; MAF 0.30 -> 51% carriers

sources_cited_consistently: Takada 2014 mouse KO (Abcg2-KO mice -> 50% reduction in intestinal flux + serum UA elevation)
Sorensen 1962 / 1965 historical tracer studies (cited by Miyazaki 2025)
intestinal_urate_excretion_fraction.load_bearing_for_comp019: HIGH — sets the stake at risk in the gut compartment. If gut goes from 33% to ~16% (50%-functional ABCG2 per the Miyazaki gradient), the daily mass balance shifts and some compensatory renal uptick is needed, but the kidney's capacity is already constrained in many gout patients.
intestinal_urate_excretion_fraction.verification_tier: Multi-source consensus + WebFetch verification of PMC8907525 review.
